calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-19-2024 Off Calendar

Declaration Of Katherine F. Wenger In Support Of Application For Court Default Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-30-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being set for hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:33 am and concluding at 10:06 am.

Plaintiff Anna Rembold is present represented by Katherine Wenger, Esq., placed under oath and testifies.
Defendant Cynthia Weldon not appearing.

Plaintiffs counsel presents summary of facts, claims and an offer of proof.

Plaintiff submits the following exhibits for the Court's review:
Exhibit 1: Photo of the property.........................ID/EVID

The matter is submitted.

By the statements made by Plaintiff and its counsel, documents filed, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence proving each element of the complaint.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Anna Rembold, and against Defendant Cynthia Weldon, as follows:

WHEREAS, based on the papers submitted by Plaintiff in support of her application of
default judgment, the Court finds the elements of Adverse Possession to have been met and
therefore enters judgment transferring title of the real property identified on Exhibit A (2930 Baker Street, San Francisco, California 94123, APN No. 0939-044, the "Property") from
Cynthia Weldon and Margaret Weldon to Anna Rembold, with the Property remaining subject to
the Deed of Trust recorded with the San Francisco County recorder?s office on April 6, 2001,
bearing Document Number 2001-G927729.

Proposed judgment signed and filed this date. (D505)
